sub_intents.clarify: Narrow a vague / cross-disciplinary ask before picking a protocol
sub_intents.explore_triage: exploration: rank cheap, high-information probes on fresh data
sub_intents.intake: Read inputs and propose project metadata
sub_intents.mid_entry: Enter an in-progress project (prior work outside RO)

sub_intents.abandon: Mark current path as a dead end
sub_intents.build_benchmark: tool_build: measure the tool against the right baseline(s)
sub_intents.build_implement: tool_build: the inner build loop — one commit/increment per pass
sub_intents.build_publish: tool_build: publish the cut release to a registry (PyPI/crates/npm/conda)
sub_intents.build_release: tool_build: cut a versioned, reproducible release + changelog
sub_intents.build_spec: tool_build: clarify what a tool must do + how 'done' is judged
sub_intents.build_test: tool_build: design the testing regime + CI gate for the tool
sub_intents.explore_probe: exploration: one probe → observe → decide cycle in scratch
sub_intents.explore_promote: exploration: graduate a probe into a real numbered step
sub_intents.hybrid_handoff: hybrid: cross the tool↔analysis boundary with the tool version pinned
sub_intents.hybrid_run: hybrid: build a tool and use it for analysis in one project, provenance spanning both
sub_intents.new_experiment: Create + run the next numbered step
sub_intents.notebook_promote: notebook: graduate a trustworthy notebook into a script / step / figure
sub_intents.notebook_reproduce: notebook: harden a notebook so anyone can re-run it (pin env, declare inputs, parameterize)
sub_intents.notebook_run: notebook: author + run a notebook with cell-provenance discipline

sub_intents.comparison: Head-to-head benchmark of N methods on one task
sub_intents.consult: Methodological consultation / explain / teach (no project commit)
sub_intents.ethics: Data ethics review (IRB / privacy / consent / fairness)
sub_intents.eval_design: Design the train/val/test split, CV, metrics, comparison test
sub_intents.fairness: Fairness audit for ML / decision systems
sub_intents.mcp_compose: Compose other MCP servers alongside Research OS (Postgres / Slack / GitHub / Figma / etc.)
sub_intents.qual_coding: Codebook / coding-scheme development for qualitative data
sub_intents.reproduce: Attempt to reproduce a published analysis
sub_intents.subfield_pipeline: Identify the specific subfield + survey its canonical pipeline
sub_intents.sweep: Design a hyperparameter sweep (search space, budget, strategy)
sub_intents.tool_setup: Install + verify external tools (Node / Quarto / Docker / R / Julia / ffmpeg / LaTeX)

sub_intents.codebook_governance: multi_study: amend the shared commons safely after studies started
sub_intents.program_setup: multi_study: shared codebook + prereg + how studies roll up
sub_intents.study_register: multi_study: register a new sub-study against the program commons

sub_intents.code: Code review of analysis scripts (yours or collaborator's)
sub_intents.figure: Critique a single figure (yours or theirs)
sub_intents.quick: Fast critical appraisal of an external paper
sub_intents.respond: Draft response to peer-review comments on YOUR paper

sub_intents.autopilot: Let the AI drive with explicit gates
sub_intents.collaborator: Hand off to a HUMAN outside Research-OS

sub_intents.animation: Animation for time-series / model behaviour / talks
sub_intents.cross_study_synthesis: multi_study: roll the studies up into one program-level claim
sub_intents.end_matter: Data / code availability + CRediT + funding + COI + ack
sub_intents.figure_arc: Order figures across a paper / talk / poster
sub_intents.humanities_essay: Non-IMRAD humanities essay (thesis + close readings + counter)
sub_intents.inputs_only: Synthesis from inputs/ alone (no in-RO analysis ran)
sub_intents.interactive: Interactive dashboard design (Observable, Streamlit, Shiny, deck.gl)
sub_intents.multi_panel: Compose a multi-panel figure (Figure 2 = A / B / C / D)
sub_intents.network: Network / graph visualization (DAGs, citation, knowledge graphs)
sub_intents.notebook_synthesize: notebook: weave notebook outputs into the requested deliverable
sub_intents.null_findings: Publishable companion for refuted / inconclusive / abandoned
sub_intents.showcase: Showcase / novel-research visualization (the visual IS the contribution)
sub_intents.slides: Talk / presentation deck (lab meeting / conference / defense)
sub_intents.title: Title workshop (single most-read sentence)
sub_intents.update: Progress update for PI / advisor / lab
sub_intents.viz_build: Build / polish a figure or figure deck (workflow)

decomposition.purpose: Full synthesis audit pass (scope='synthesis', dimension='all').
Verify citations resolve / are not hallucinated (scope='project', dimension='citations').
Audit each figure (scope='step', dimension='figure').

audit/audit_and_validation.summary: Full audit pass on a synthesis output.
triggers: is this paper ready to submit

decomposition.purpose: Artefact + paper + figure + end-matter + cover-letter + ethics + repro + claims + venue checks.
Full synthesis audit pass (scope='synthesis', dimension='all').
Audit every number traces to a workspace output (scope='project', dimension='claims').

audit/pre_submission_checklist.summary: Final ready-to-submit gate (GREEN / YELLOW / RED + punch list).

decomposition.purpose: Enumerate outputs, check sibling .prov.json existence, validate fields, fix missing by re-running the figure script (which should drop the provenance sidecar) under tool_python_exec so the provenance hook records the run.
Re-run any artefact-producing script under provenance to backfill missing sidecars.

audit/provenance_completeness.summary: Per-output provenance sidecar coverage — every figure/table/model artefact must ship with a `<stem>.prov.json` (script + git SHA + RNG seed + lib versions + params + wall-time).
triggers: show me how this figure was made

decomposition.purpose: State the claim, pick the baseline + metric, hold conditions fair, measure with uncertainty, interpret against acceptance.
Run the benchmark harness (tool_task for long sweeps); write harness + results into eval/.
Log the performance claim-with-evidence (baseline + metric + uncertainty).

build/benchmark_vs_baseline.summary: Measure the tool honestly against the right baseline(s): the metric that matches its purpose, fair fixed conditions (same inputs/hardware/seeds), and reported uncertainty.

decomposition.purpose: Carry provenance forward, pin the proven version, integrate behind a seam, test through it, set a version-change policy, commit with provenance behind a checkpoint.
Pin the dependency at the exact proven version in the manifest/lockfile.
Confirm the dependency tree + surface transitive surprises.
Snapshot the integration boundary so it can be rolled back if it goes wrong.
Tie gap → scout → spike → integrated version into one durable record.

build/dependency_integration.summary: Promote a proven candidate into the real build: carry the spike provenance forward, pin the exact proven version + record licence, wire it in behind a seam, test through the seam, set a version-change + re-proof policy, and commit with provenance behind a rollback checkpoint.
triggers: make it part of the tool

decomposition.purpose: git status / git log in the inner repo to read the working tree before the increment.
Implement the scoped increment in the inner repo.
Run the test that proves the increment (or tool_r_exec / tool_julia_exec / tool_bash_exec per language).
Run the build + full test suite + linters; then git add + git commit with a message linked to the objective.
Record the committed increment + tick the milestone.

build/implement_iteration.summary: The inner build loop — scope one increment, implement it in the inner repo, prove it with a test, run the checks, commit against the objective. Loops per increment.

decomposition.purpose: Set the falsifiable bar, pin + snapshot, build a throwaway spike on a real (hashed) input, judge, checkpoint the verdict, decide its fate.
Capture the interpreter + dependency versions the spike ran under.
Run the throwaway spike in the scratch surface, never in the real tree.
Snapshot the spike + env at the decision moment so it's re-runnable / reversible.
Record (version, input-hash, bar, result, verdict).

build/integration_spike.summary: Prove a scouted candidate works before building on it: set a falsifiable pass/fail bar, pin the exact version + snapshot the env, run a throwaway spike on a real input (hashed), judge against the bar, checkpoint the verdict, and route adopt / reject / needs-more.

decomposition.purpose: Frame the gap, scout candidates, rank by value vs risk, decide what gets spiked vs built in-house.
Surface the algorithm / technique / published method behind the gap.
Surface candidate libraries / frameworks / reference implementations.
Record the scout outcome, the pick, and the rejection reasons.

build/method_scouting.summary: Scout external methods / papers / code worth adopting for a build: frame the capability gap, collect candidates with provenance + licence + integration cost, rank by value vs risk, and decide what to spike (or to build in-house).
triggers: is there a method for this
is there a library for this
is there a paper for this
what's the state of the art

decomposition.purpose: Decide registry, complete metadata + licence, verify a clean-env install, gate the publish, verify the public install.
Build the artefact, install into a clean env, publish (staging first), verify the published install.
Record the publish (name + version + registry + clean-install verified).

build/package_and_publish.summary: Distribute a cut release so others can install it: decide whether + where to publish, complete the packaging metadata + licence, verify a clean-environment install, then gate the irreversible publish to the registry.

decomposition.purpose: Confirm releasable, decide the version, write the changelog from history, update docs, pin + record the env, tag + build the artefact.
git log since last release, version bump, build, git tag, build the distributable artefact.
Snapshot the build environment for reproducibility.
Record the release (version + headline + tag).

build/release_and_changelog.summary: Cut a versioned, reproducible release: SemVer bump from what changed for users, changelog from the commit history, updated docs, pinned deps + recorded build env, tag + citable artefact.

decomposition.purpose: Name the consumer, write acceptance criteria, pin the interface contract, ground + record load-bearing decisions as ADRs.
Surface candidate libraries/frameworks for a sub-problem before committing.
Write spec/requirements.md, spec/design.md, decisions/NNNN-*.md.
Log each load-bearing design decision with its rationale + grounding.

build/spec_and_design.summary: Clarify what a tool must do + how its success is judged; record acceptance criteria, the interface contract, and load-bearing decisions as ADRs.

decomposition.purpose: Map the failure surface, choose + justify test kinds, set a defensible coverage stance + CI gate.
Scaffold the test layout + CI config and run the suite once to confirm it's green.
Log the testing-regime decision so per-increment tests inherit a consistent target.

build/test_strategy.summary: Reason about the testing regime that fits THIS tool (unit/integration/property/golden/fuzz/benchmark), a defensible coverage stance, and what the CI gate enforces.

decomposition.purpose: Define the bar, hypothesise weak spots, run fixed-condition evaluations, rank by evidenced impact, feed back + re-measure.
Run the evaluation experiments (tool_task for long sweeps); write scripts + results into the eval reports.
Log each evaluation round + the chosen next increment so the tool's development history is auditable.

build/tool_evaluation_loop.summary: Probe the tool to find where it falls short, rank an evidence-based improvement backlog, feed the top fix back to the build loop, re-measure. The iterative evaluate→improve heartbeat.
triggers: what's the biggest problem with the tool

domain/domain_analysis.summary: Classify domain + bias profile + reporting standard.

domain/research_design.summary: Pick study design (RCT / cohort / cross-sectional / qual / sim).

decomposition.purpose: Frame the question, probe in scratch, record the observation, decide promote / drop / iterate.
Write + run the cheap throw-away probe in the sandbox.
Append the question + observation to workspace/scratch/probe_log.md.

exploration/exploration_loop.summary: The exploration-mode home loop: frame a question cheaply → run a scratch probe → record the observation → decide promote / drop / iterate.

decomposition.purpose: Confirm worth promoting, register the hypothesis, create the step, carry probe artefacts in, back-fill rigor.
Register the hypothesis the probe hinted at.
Create the numbered step folder the probe graduates into.
Log the promotion (probe question → step).

exploration/exploration_promote.summary: Graduate a scratch probe that earned it into a real numbered step — back-filling the hypothesis, method grounding, and provenance exploration deferred.
triggers: keep this and do it properly

decomposition.purpose: Read the landscape, surface intent, rank cheap high-information probes, hand the top one to the loop.
Cheap landscape read of shape / dtypes / missingness.
Write the ranked probe backlog to workspace/scratch/triage.md.

exploration/exploration_triage.summary: Orient on fresh data and produce a short, ranked list of cheap, high-information probes for the exploration loop to run first.
triggers: what should i poke at first

decomposition.purpose: Create the next numbered experiment folder.
Ground methodology before coding.
Decompose the step into atomic sub-tasks.
Write scripts/<NN>_<slug>_v1.<ext>.
Run the script (or tool_r_exec / tool_julia_exec / tool_bash_exec).
Log method + parameters + justification.
Write conclusions.md for the step.

guidance/analysis_plan.summary: Iterative experiment loop — create step, script, audit, conclude.

decomposition.purpose: Load gates + heartbeat policy.
Start executing under autopilot.

guidance/autopilot.summary: Drive the project hands-off; explicit gates only.

decomposition.purpose: Confirm casual mode + create lightweight step.

guidance/casual_exploration.summary: Lightweight mode — skip reproducibility paperwork, sandbox-first.

decomposition.purpose: Snapshot workspace as a rollback target.
Write the handoff doc with running tasks, hypotheses, dead-end lessons.

guidance/chat_handoff.summary: Hand off cleanly to another chat / AI / collaborator.

guidance/code_review.summary: Review analysis scripts for correctness / reproducibility / leakage.

guidance/collaboration_handoff.summary: Package the project for a HUMAN collaborator outside Research-OS.

decomposition.purpose: Detect, classify severity, voice, respect, log, surface in synthesis if relevant.

guidance/constructive_disagreement.summary: Structured AI pushback when the AI's evidence disagrees with the researcher's direction.
triggers: tell me if i am wrong
be a tough reviewer of my plan
play devil's advocate on this plan

decomposition.purpose: Mark folder as __DEAD_END with rationale.
Capture the lesson for future steps.
Propose alternative direction.

guidance/dead_end_routing.summary: Abandon a path cleanly + decide where to go from here.

guidance/hypothesis_tracking.summary: Add / update / list hypotheses (registry across steps).

decomposition.purpose: Survey state + search + propose options.
Log the chosen direction.

guidance/iterative_planning.summary: AI proposes the next best step based on state + literature + tools.

decomposition.purpose: Classify entry archetype + route to the right downstream protocol.

guidance/mid_pipeline_entry.summary: Enter Research-OS with work already done outside it (route past redundant intake).
triggers: i have a draft just need
i have a draft, just need
we've been working on this for months
i have the analysis i need the writing
i have the writing i need the figures

guidance/peer_review_response.summary: Structured response to external peer-review comments on YOUR paper.

decomposition.purpose: Route any loose files into inputs/ first.
Infer domain + question + hypotheses from inputs.
Confirm registered hypotheses + new docs.

guidance/project_startup.summary: First substantive protocol: intake + classify + question lock-in.
triggers: tell me what you're going to do
i have a csv what do i do
i have a dataset what do i do
i have some data what do i do
what do i do with this data
what do i do with my data
where do i start with this data

decomposition.purpose: Stage the review skeleton.
Walk through the appraisal.

guidance/quick_paper_review.summary: Fast 20-40 min critical appraisal of a paper.
triggers: what do you think of this paper

decomposition.purpose: Ingest reviews, parse to grid, classify + route per comment, execute routes, re-audit, fill response, assemble letter.
Generate the formal point-by-point letter skeleton (operation='response').
Re-audit every number in the revised manuscript (scope='project', dimension='claims').
Re-audit the revised prose for hedging / causal-language / reading level (scope='project', dimension='prose').
Master re-audit before pre-submission gate.

guidance/revise_and_resubmit.summary: End-to-end R&R orchestration — intake → triage → route per comment (analysis/writing/figure) → response-grid → re-audit → point-by-point letter.
triggers: revise the paper based on reviews

decomposition.purpose: Classify ambiguity → ask one narrowing question → re-route.

guidance/scope_clarification.summary: Narrow a vague / open-ended / cross-disciplinary ask into a workable scope BEFORE picking a protocol.
triggers: help me figure out where to start
i'm not sure what this is
this might be x or y
is this even the right tool
i'm stuck before i even start
i don't know what to ask

decomposition.purpose: Load state + config + history + dep inventory + next protocol in one call.

decomposition.decision: Else greet researcher and wait for their first ask.

guidance/session_boot.summary: First protocol of every session. Loads state + config + sets behaviour.

decomposition.purpose: Reconstruct intent + status in one call.
Confirm pipeline-recommended next step.

guidance/session_resume.summary: Re-enter a paused / interrupted / handed-off project.

decomposition.purpose: Identify the bottleneck half, advance it, decide when to cross, keep the cross-boundary provenance thread.
Log which half advanced + any boundary crossing this pass.

hybrid/hybrid_workflow.summary: The hybrid-mode home loop: hold one project that both builds a tool and uses it for analysis, decide which half is the bottleneck each pass, and keep a provenance thread spanning both halves.
triggers: build a tool and use it
build it and analyze with it
build it and analyse with it
develop the method and apply it
i'm building the thing i'm studying

decomposition.purpose: Name the direction, confirm the increment is ready, pin + record the version (or triage the finding into a tool change), carry the narrative.
Log the crossing (analysis step ↔ tool version dependency / feedback).

hybrid/tool_to_analysis_handoff.summary: Cross the tool↔analysis boundary deliberately: pin the exact tool version an analysis depends on (forward), or triage an analysis finding into a tool change and flag affected past steps (backward).
triggers: use the tool for the analysis
the tool is ready, run it
feed this back into the tool
the analysis shows the tool is wrong
the analysis needs the tool to change

decomposition.purpose: Scope dimensions, ingest each paper, build matrix, name common ground + disagreements.

literature/comparative_paper_review.summary: Compare-and-contrast 2-N papers (journal club / related work / reviewer-asked / foundational).
triggers: papers x vs y vs z

decomposition.purpose: Find + download + cite + write structured comparison per claim.
Gate before path_finalize (scope='step', dimension='literature'): blocks if findings_vs_literature.md missing or DISAGREES lacks discussion.

literature/literature_per_step.summary: After a step writes Findings, search → download → cite → write findings_vs_literature.md (AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|DEFERRED) → register grounding. Gated by tool_audit (scope='step', dimension='literature').

literature/literature_search.summary: Broad literature pass on the research question.

methodology/ablation_study.summary: Component-by-component ablation of a model or pipeline.

methodology/bayesian_analysis.summary: Bayesian workflow: priors → posterior → diagnostics → checks → sensitivity.

methodology/bootstrapping_design.summary: Pick resampling scheme + interval method + B for non-parametric inference.

methodology/causal_inference_deep.summary: DAG + dowhy / IPW / DiD / RDD pipeline.

methodology/coding_scheme_development.summary: Build + iterate the qualitative codebook before full coding.

decomposition.purpose: Detect Cox fit, run Schoenfeld + log-log + per-covariate tests, route to fallback on violation.
Run the diagnostics script (lifelines / sksurv / statsmodels) or tool_r_exec for survival::coxph.
Record the PH-assumption verdict alongside the other assumption checks (scope='step', dimension='assumptions').

methodology/cox_ph_diagnostics.summary: Cox PH diagnostics — Schoenfeld residual test (Grambsch-Therneau) + log-log plot + per-covariate p-values + fallback strategy if violated.

decomposition.purpose: Walk IRB / consent / privacy / sharing / fairness / dual-use; produce verdict.

methodology/data_ethics_review.summary: IRB / consent / privacy / sharing / fairness / dual-use review on data.
triggers: is this data ethical to use

methodology/data_management_plan.summary: Funder-compliant Data Management Plan (NIH DMSP, NSF, Wellcome, ERC) with FAIR alignment.

decomposition.purpose: Reason + record each transform from raw to the analysis-ready table; raw stays immutable.

methodology/data_preparation.summary: Raw → analysis-ready: reshape / join / recode / type / outlier / filter, every decision recorded + re-runnable.
triggers: the data needs cleaning before i can analyse it
the data needs cleaning before i can analyze it

decomposition.purpose: Schema + completeness + distribution + duplicate + leakage + bias checks.

methodology/data_quality_audit.summary: Standalone data quality audit (schema / missingness / leakage / bias check).

decomposition.purpose: Identify subfield + survey + propose pipeline + assumption matrix + optional alternative branch.
Confidence-gated alternative pipeline scan (only branches if literature supports a meaningfully better path).

methodology/deep_domain_research.summary: Identify the SPECIFIC subfield + survey its canonical pipeline (≥3 cited sources); writes docs/pipeline_plan.md + assumption_matrix.md before any stats are picked.
triggers: what tools do people actually use

decomposition.purpose: Split, CV, metric set, uncertainty, paired test, freeze.

methodology/evaluation_design.summary: Design split / CV / metric set / paired comparison test for a modelling task.

decomposition.purpose: Pre-register scope, profile, build univariate + bivariate views.

methodology/exploratory_data_analysis.summary: Real exploratory data analysis + hypothesis generation (distinct from confirmatory EDA inside analysis_plan).

methodology/external_tool_setup.summary: Install + verify top-tier external tools (Node / Quarto / Docker / R / Julia / ffmpeg / LaTeX) for the active workflow.

methodology/fairness_audit.summary: Group / intersectional fairness audit for ML + decision systems.

decomposition.purpose: Identify params that matter, pick strategy, define budget, set pruning, pin seeds.

methodology/hyperparameter_search_design.summary: Design a hyperparameter sweep (search space, budget, strategy, early-stopping, seeds).

methodology/inter_rater_reliability.summary: Design + compute + report inter-rater reliability (kappa / alpha / ICC).

methodology/interview_guide_design.summary: Design the interview / topic guide before qualitative data collection.

methodology/machine_learning.summary: TRIPOD-AI ML pipeline (split, baseline, tune, eval, interpret).

methodology/mcp_ecosystem_integration.summary: Compose other MCP servers alongside Research OS — Postgres / Slack / GitHub / Notion / Figma / etc.

decomposition.purpose: Pre-register evaluation, implement each method, compare with uncertainty.

methodology/method_comparison.summary: Head-to-head benchmark of N candidate methods on the same task.

decomposition.purpose: Literature pass + layered explanation + reading list + next step.

methodology/methodological_consultation.summary: Teach / explain / compare methods without committing to a project (intuition → mechanics → caveats).

methodology/methodology_selection.summary: Choose statistical / computational method given design + data.

decomposition.purpose: Quantify missingness, justify mechanism, pick handling, run primary + sensitivity, pre-register if confirmatory.
Profile missingness per column / row / stratum.
Run MICE / IPW / pattern-mixture scripts.
Register the sensitivity spec under a different missingness mechanism (operation='define') then execute (operation='run').

methodology/missing_data_strategy.summary: Missing-data strategy — MCAR/MAR/MNAR reasoning, primary handling (complete-case or multiple imputation via MICE), mandatory sensitivity under a different mechanism.

methodology/mixed_language_orchestration.summary: Compose Python ↔ R ↔ bash within one step: hand-off files, serialization format, schema assertions, pipeline.yaml language tags.

methodology/mixed_methods.summary: Concurrent or sequential qual + quant integration.

methodology/multiple_comparisons.summary: Pick FWER vs FDR and the correction method per hypothesis family.

methodology/pick_tool_stack.summary: Pick language + library per sub-task (R Bioconductor for DE, Python scanpy for scRNA, R survival for Cox PH, etc.) — sister to figure_guidelines.pick_library at the analysis level.

methodology/pilot_study.summary: Small-N feasibility / variance estimation before the full study.

decomposition.purpose: Source effect size, name test, build power table + curve, justify N.

methodology/power_analysis.summary: Standalone power / sample-size justification (prospective / post-hoc sensitivity / sequential).

methodology/preregistration.summary: Freeze SAP + hypotheses BEFORE data analysis; diff at synthesis.
triggers: before i look at the data

methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction.summary: HIPAA / IRB / GDPR-safe transcript redaction BEFORE coding (presidio + spaCy NER + regex; deterministic pseudonym map).

decomposition.purpose: Walk saturation curve, reflexivity, intercoder agreement, member checking, quote anonymisation, audit trail.
Compute saturation curve + Cohen's κ / Krippendorff's α on shared coding subset.

methodology/qualitative_quality_audit.summary: Qualitative quality audit — COREQ + SRQR enforcement (saturation, reflexivity, intercoder agreement ≥ 0.7, member checking, anonymisation, audit trail).

methodology/replication_study.summary: Direct or conceptual replication of a published analysis.

decomposition.purpose: Inventory provided assets, pin env, rerun, compare numbers.

methodology/reproduction_attempt.summary: Attempt to reproduce a published analysis (rerun the authors' pipeline).
triggers: can i get the same numbers
redo the analysis from the paper

methodology/spatial_analysis.summary: Analyse spatial data — CRS, spatial dependence/autocorrelation, spatial-vs-aspatial model choice, MAUP/edge effects, spatial cross-validation. The analysis counterpart to map plotting.

methodology/survey_design.summary: Design / draft a new survey instrument before collecting data.

methodology/tool_discovery.summary: Find candidate libraries / CLIs for a task.

methodology/uncertainty_quantification.summary: Calibrated predictive uncertainty (conformal / temperature / quantile / ensemble).

decomposition.purpose: Decide the durable form, extract reusable logic / create the step, trace artefacts, hand off.
Create the numbered step when promoting a notebook to an analysis step.
Record the promotion (notebook → script / step / figure).

notebook/notebook_promote.summary: Graduate a trustworthy notebook into a durable form — a reusable script, a numbered analysis step, or a traced figure feeding synthesis — re-applying the rigor a notebook makes easy to skip.
triggers: turn this notebook into a script
make this notebook a real step

decomposition.purpose: Pin the env, declare inputs, make deterministic, parameterize, verify against a fresh kernel.
Snapshot the interpreter + library versions into an environment file.
Restart-and-run-all from a clean kernel; run headless with default params.
Record what makes the notebook reproducible (env file, inputs, params).

notebook/notebook_reproduce.summary: Harden a notebook so anyone can re-run it: pin the environment, declare every input path, make execution deterministic, lift hand-tuned values into a parameters cell, verify against a clean kernel.

decomposition.purpose: Inventory outputs, select for the story, qualify each input, hand to the synthesis engine.
Record the selected outputs + the deliverable built.

notebook/notebook_synthesize.summary: Weave the figures, tables, and findings the notebooks produced into the requested deliverable (paper / report / dashboard / slides), selecting honestly and qualifying every input before synthesis.
triggers: turn these notebooks into a paper
turn the notebooks into a report
make the slides from the notebooks

decomposition.purpose: Scope the notebook, structure cells for provenance, restart-and-run-all, persist derived outputs, document + decide.
Author + run notebook cells; validate with a clean-kernel run.
Log the notebook's headline finding for later synthesis.

notebook/notebook_workflow.summary: Author + run a notebook with cell-provenance discipline: one idea per cell, seed in cell 1, restart-and-run-all validity, outputs derived not hand-edited.

decomposition.purpose: Assess blast radius, version the change, decide re-code vs crosswalk, propagate to studies.
Write the versioned codebook amendment + changelog into shared/.
Record the codebook amendment (version + affected studies + decision).

program/codebook_governance.summary: Amend the shared commons safely after studies have started coding: assess blast radius across studies, version the change, and decide re-coding vs crosswalk so the codebook stays a true common denominator.
triggers: add a code to the program

decomposition.purpose: Check commensurability, choose pool vs narrative, treat heterogeneity as signal, qualify the claim.
Record the program-level synthesis (method + heterogeneity + claim strength).

program/cross_study_synthesis.summary: Roll the completed studies up into one program-level claim: confirm they're commensurable, choose pooling vs narrative honestly, examine heterogeneity as a finding, and qualify the synthesis to the evidence.
triggers: what do the studies say together

decomposition.purpose: Define the shared codebook, frame the program prereg, plan the roll-up, scaffold the first study.
Write shared/codebook.md, shared/preregistration.md, roll_up/README.md.
Scaffold the first sub-study under studies/.
Record the program-setup decision (shared commons frozen).

program/program_setup.summary: Establish the program commons before any study runs: the shared codebook every study codes to, the program-level preregistration, and how the studies roll up.

decomposition.purpose: Bind the study to the codebook + prereg, declare its roll-up contribution, scaffold it harmonized.
Scaffold the new sub-study directory under studies/.
Record the study registration (bound to commons vX).

program/study_register.summary: Register a new sub-study against the program commons: bind it to the shared codebook + program prereg, declare what it contributes to the roll-up, and scaffold it so it harmonizes by construction.

reproducibility/reproducibility.summary: Snapshot env + verify everything reruns clean.

synthesis/defense_prep.summary: Anticipate + rehearse Q&A for dissertation defense / job talk / high-stakes talk.

synthesis/deliverable_design.summary: The visual-design skill: design a custom shareable deliverable (dashboard/poster/slides) — pick + compose a layout archetype + professional palette + type system for an external reader. Not one fixed template.

decomposition.purpose: Write synthesis/essay.typ with the six interpretive sections + AI markers.
Inspect available sources for the critical conversation + close readings.
Audit substantiveness + cliches once the AI fills in the sections.
Render essay.typ → essay.pdf with the humanities_essay venue template.

synthesis/humanities_essay_structure.summary: AI authors a non-IMRAD essay.typ (thesis → context → close readings → critical conversation → counter-argument → stakes). Compiles via humanities_essay.typ template.

synthesis/journal_selection.summary: Compare 3-5 candidate venues on scope / evidence / cost / timeline / open-science fit.

synthesis/manuscript_outline.summary: Outline + storyboard the paper BEFORE drafting (figures-first narrative).

decomposition.purpose: Pick format (poster | handout), audience, headline, figure(s), QR, build, audit.

synthesis/printable.summary: Print-first deliverable (poster | handout | one_pager). Replaces synthesis_handout + synthesis_poster via the `format` parameter.

decomposition.purpose: Build the 7-persona pre-submission simulation brief (operation='simulate').
Scaffold one rebuttal markdown per substantive comment (operation='rebuttal', comment=..., persona=...).
Assemble rebuttals into response_to_reviewers.md + Typst PDF (operation='compile').
Audit rebuttals for hand-waving + missing evidence (scope='synthesis', dimension='reviewer_responses').

synthesis/reviewer_response.summary: Pre-submission 7-persona adversarial self-review + per-comment rebuttal scaffold + audit. Internal dry-run companion to guidance/peer_review_response.

synthesis/synthesis_abstract.summary: AI authors a standalone abstract from the finished workspace.

decomposition.purpose: Scope, extract significance, justify fit, draft, source reviewers.

synthesis/synthesis_cover_letter.summary: Draft journal cover letter (fit + significance + reviewers + disclosures).
triggers: letter to the editor for submission

decomposition.purpose: Build a shadow step + provenance manifest, then run target synthesis.

synthesis/synthesis_from_inputs.summary: Synthesis from inputs/ alone — when prior analyses were done outside Research-OS.
triggers: synthesize from what i already have
i have results write the paper
migrate this old project into a paper

synthesis/synthesis_grant.summary: AI authors grant.typ (R01 / NSF / Wellcome / etc.).

decomposition.purpose: Loaded via redirect from synthesis_handout: format=handout.

synthesis/synthesis_handout.summary: Single-page printable handout / one-pager (5 audience profiles, QR code mandatory). Redirects to synthesis/printable(format=handout).
triggers: for the audience to take away

decomposition.purpose: Pick lay audience + translate jargon + anchor numbers.

synthesis/synthesis_lay_summary.summary: Plain-language summary for non-experts (press / public / patient / funder / blog / social).

synthesis/synthesis_null_findings.summary: Publishable companion for refuted / inconclusive / underpowered / abandoned.

synthesis/synthesis_paper.summary: AI authors IMRAD paper.typ; tool_typst_compile renders PDF.

synthesis/synthesis_poster.summary: AI authors poster.typ; tool_typst_compile renders PDF. Redirects to synthesis/printable(format=poster).

decomposition.purpose: Mechanical state snapshot to feed the update.
Compose the audience-tailored update.

synthesis/synthesis_progress_update.summary: Short progress update for PI / advisor / lab / collaborator / stand-up.
triggers: what should i tell my advisor

synthesis/synthesis_report.summary: AI authors a technical / executive report.

decomposition.purpose: Pick audience + outline + design principles.

synthesis/synthesis_slides.summary: Build a presentation deck (lab meeting / conference / defense / invited / teaching).

synthesis/synthesis_step_report.summary: AI designs a self-contained visual artefact for ONE step (meeting-grade).
triggers: page for what i just did
show this step at the meeting

decomposition.purpose: Extract search terms, generate alternatives, substring-test, shortlist, stress-test.

synthesis/synthesis_title_workshop.summary: Workshop a title (≥6 alternatives across archetypes, substring test, shortlist).

visualization/animation_design.summary: Animate a figure for time series / model behaviour / talks (static fallback mandatory).

decomposition.purpose: Extract palette, simulate, contrast, grayscale, redundant-encoding, fix.

visualization/color_accessibility_audit.summary: Color-blind simulation (3 types) + WCAG contrast + grayscale + redundant-encoding audit.
triggers: is this readable for colour-blind readers

visualization/distribution_comparison.summary: Compare distributions across groups (raincloud / halfeye / ridgeline / beeswarm — beyond bar+error).

decomposition.purpose: Walk the chart-family / encoding / caption critique.

visualization/figure_critique.summary: Reviewer-style critique of a single figure (yours or theirs).
triggers: the reviewers complained about my figure
how would you improve this figure

visualization/figure_guidelines.summary: Picking the right chart type and styling for publication.

decomposition.purpose: Inventory, name story, cut-test, assign-to-arc, brief the drafter.

visualization/figure_narrative_arc.summary: Order figures across a paper / talk / poster (figure budget + arc + cut decisions).

visualization/geospatial_visualization.summary: Plot a map (choropleth, points, raster); projection + classification + palette.

visualization/interactive_dashboard_desi.summary: Design a multi-page interactive dashboard (Observable / Streamlit / Shiny / Dash / Panel / kepler.gl / deck.gl) — next tier beyond static HTML.

visualization/interactive_figure_design.summary: Design ONE interactive figure (hover, brush, zoom, lasso) as a companion to a static PNG/SVG. Plotly / Altair / mpld3 / pyvis / igv.js / cellxgene / glimma — pick by data type. Static fallback REQUIRED.

decomposition.purpose: Name the story, design the grid, build panels, compose, caption.

visualization/multi_panel_composition.summary: Compose a multi-panel figure (Figure 2 = A / B / C / D) with shared scales + combined caption.

visualization/network_visualization.summary: Plot a network / graph (DAGs, citation networks, knowledge graphs); layout + encoding reasoning.

visualization/showcase_visualization.summary: Top-tier visualization where the visual IS the contribution (HCI / data art / journal covers / novel research). Three.js / D3 / WebGL / Observable.

visualization/uncertainty_visualization.summary: Visualize intervals / fans / ensembles / posteriors / calibration honestly.

decomposition.purpose: Scope + plan figures, then build via an AI-authored plotting script (see visualization/figure_guidelines).
Audit each figure (scope='step', dimension='figure_full').

visualization/visualization_workflow.summary: Build / polish a figure or figure deck without committing to the full analysis_plan loop.

decomposition.purpose: Gather genuine samples, observe voice dimensions, distil a profile, reconcile with venue/rigor, apply + confirm.

writing/voice_calibration.summary: Learn the researcher's own writing voice from their samples + apply it to drafts (author-voice, not venue-voice). Profile flows into writing_core.
triggers: this doesn't sound like my writing

writing/writing_analysis_log.summary: Format for structured entries in workspace/analysis.md.

writing/writing_citations.summary: Maintain workspace/citations.md so every claim is grounded.

writing/writing_core.summary: Universal writing rules. Implicitly loaded by every synthesis.

decomposition.purpose: Draft each end-matter section per journal convention.

writing/writing_data_availability.summary: Draft end matter — data + code availability, CRediT, funding, COI, acknowledgements.

decomposition.purpose: Pull evidence floor + draft per-section discipline.
Audit prose for hedging / causal-language / reading level (scope='project', dimension='prose').
Audit every number traces to a workspace output (scope='project', dimension='claims').

writing/writing_discussion.summary: Draft the Discussion section (principal findings, alternative explanations, limitations route).

decomposition.purpose: Enumerate from categories, pair with implications, prioritise.

writing/writing_limitations.summary: Draft the Limitations section (no boilerplate; every limitation paired with implication).

writing/writing_methods.summary: How to document methods in workspace/methods.md.

decomposition.purpose: Build evidence table, draft per-structure, audit causal-language + number-format.
Audit every number traces to a workspace output (scope='project', dimension='claims').

writing/writing_results.summary: Draft the Results section (report numbers, defer interpretation, full statistical form).

triggers: are my figures up to date
